LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF EVELYN POQUEZ' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. GRANTING PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO FILE A FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT SERVE THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS ADD NOTHING TO THE EXISTING COMPLAINT AND THEREFORE DENIAL OF THIS MOTION DOES NOT PREVENT PLAINTIFF FROM PRESENTING A MERITORIOUS CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS SUNCOR HOLDINGS-COPII, LLC, SUNCOR HOLDINGS, LLC, GSM PARTNERS, LLC AND PACIFIC TBG, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1; SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD EITHER THAT DEFENDANTS ARE PLAINTIFF'S FRANCHISOR OR THE PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTED TO EXERCISE ITS CONTRACTUAL RIGHTS IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Russell F. Brasso, Esq. of Foreman & Brasso, Tel. 415.433.3475, for Plaintiff and moving party;
Bradley H. Kreshnek, Esq. of Freedman & Taitelman, Tel. 310.201.0005, for Defendant and opposing party via court call.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EVELYN POQUEZ' Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint is Denied. Granting Plaintiff leave to file a Fourth Amended Complaint does not serve the interests of justice. The proposed amendments add nothing to the existing complaint and therefore denial of this motion does not prevent Plaintiff from presenting a meritorious cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SUNCOR HOLDINGS-COP II, LLC, SUNCOR HOLDINGS, LLC, GSM PARTNERS, LLC and PACIFIC TBG, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Bradley H. Kreshek of Freedman & Taitelman, LLP (310.201.0005) for defendants and moving parties; Russell F. Brasso of Foreman & Brasso (433.3475) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, there is a triable issue whether defendants acted with due diligence and made good faith best efforts to obtain the permits and entitlements in a reasonably prompt manner. Defendants fail to demonstrate that plaintiff cannot establish causation and defendants' own architect indicated that the permits could be obtained in 6 to 8 months. The contract does not limit plaintiff's right to recover monetary damages. As to the second cause of action, there is a triable issue whether defendants repudiated plaintiff's option rights by selling the property prior to giving plaintiff a reasonable time to exercise those rights. The third cause of action adds nothing substantive legally or factually beyond the first two claims and is thus superfluous. As to the fourth cause of action, defendants sustained their burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether Suncor was a franchisor or an affiliate of a franchisor. Orders signed on the summary judgment motion and the parties' evidentiary objections. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
